medications.drug_name: short-course hypnotic (e.g. temazepam or a Z-drug) — brief, cautious, in-person
medications.indication_rationale: A brief course (a few nights) can bridge acute transient insomnia, but sedative-hypnotics carry dependence/tolerance and next-day-impairment risks and should be prescribed after an in-person assessment (including a mania/safety screen), not as first-line. Source used a 3-night supply of temazepam 10mg at bedtime only.
medications.dose_route_frequency: if prescribed after in-person review: lowest effective dose at bedtime for only a few nights (e.g. temazepam 10mg nocte x ~3) (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Regular/prolonged hypnotic use causes dependence, tolerance and rebound insomnia — keep any course strictly short.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Sleep hygiene / stimulus control: a 'digital sunset' (no screens ~1 hour before bed), keep the bed for sleep only, if not asleep in ~20 minutes get up and do a quiet activity in dim light until sleepy, consistent wake time, avoid caffeine/alcohol; consider CBT-i.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Behavioural measures are first-line and reduce the hyperarousal driving the insomnia.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Address the stressor: practical support after the job loss, and psychological support/counselling as needed.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Treating the precipitant is key to resolution.

patient_education_points.point: This looks like short-term insomnia brought on by the stress of losing your job — very understandable and usually settles within days once things ease and your sleep routine is re-set. The most effective approach is sleep-hygiene/behavioural steps rather than sleeping tablets.

Try a 'digital sunset' (no screens for an hour before bed), keep the bed for sleep only, get up if you can't sleep after ~20 minutes, and keep a consistent wake time. Any sleeping tablet should only be for a few nights and needs an in-person review first. Don't drive while this exhausted.

This is a sensitive time — if you have any thoughts of harming yourself or that life isn't worth living, or you feel you can't keep yourself safe, please reach out for help immediately (call 000 or a crisis line); I can help you find the right support. Also seek review if you start feeling unusually 'high'/energised without needing sleep.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Sleep, mood and safety; the stressor; taper/stop any hypnotic; consider CBT-i / psychological support.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Any self-harm thoughts or not feeling safe — urgent help/crisis line/000; manic features — urgent mental-health review.

must_include_items: Sensitively screen suicidality/safety and mania (bipolar) — a mental-health assessment, not just a sedative
Sleep hygiene / stimulus control / CBT-i as first-line + stressor support
Any hypnotic strictly short-term and after in-person review (dependence risk)
Self-harm / mania safety-net with an offer to help find support/resources
acceptable_alternatives: CBT-i, sleep-hygiene measures; a brief hypnotic (benzodiazepine or Z-drug) short-term if appropriate after review; melatonin in some contexts
errors_of_commission: Prescribing/recommending a hypnotic without a safety and mania screen or in-person review
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires a sensitive suicidality/safety AND mania screen, behavioural first-line management, any hypnotic only briefly/after review, and a self-harm/mania safety-net.
